title: poa nemoralis var. prorepens
intro: Poa nemoralis var. prorepens, also known as creeping meadow-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that grows low to the ground, typically found in wetlands and damp meadows. The medicinal properties of Poa nemoralis var. prorepens include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, and it has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits, such as reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
